semanticScore.callRationale: Started the Tavily trajectory, inspected the schema exposed by the harness, selected tavily_search because it had native include_domains source-policy control, and searched for the four required Firecrawl Search API fields with include_domains set to docs.firecrawl.dev and include_raw_content enabled. No extraction call was needed because the first result was the official Search API endpoint and contained direct evidence for categories, country, timeout, and ignoreInvalidURLs.

retrievalQuality.rationale: The top result was the official Firecrawl Search API docs page at docs.firecrawl.dev/api-reference/endpoint/search and it directly covered all expected terms. All returned URLs were from docs.firecrawl.dev, so the source policy was satisfied.

agentExperience.rationale: The Tavily schema made the search tool and include_domains control obvious. The search returned the needed answer in one call, but include_raw_content produced a large response with duplicated endpoint content and extra localized CLI pages, so the output was useful but noisier than ideal.

strengths: Native include_domains control allowed an honest docs.firecrawl.dev-only source policy.
The first result was the exact official Search API endpoint and contained all required fields.
One search call was enough; no follow-up fetch was necessary.
weaknesses: Raw content was large and duplicated parts of the API page.
The result set included localized CLI documentation pages that were source-policy compliant but not needed for the final answer.
